Admissions
HomeAcademics

Academics

Scholastic Assessment System

At Sree Jayam School (SJS), student learning is evaluated through a structured Scholastic Assessment System that measures academic progress, understanding, and overall performance throughout the academic year. The assessment system is designed according to the academic stages of learning and includes unit tests, term examinations, and periodic evaluations. The purpose of these assessments is to help students develop strong academic skills, improve their performance, and provide teachers and parents with a clear understanding of the student's progress.

Foundation Stage (PS–I to Grade II)

In the Foundation Stage, the focus is on building strong basic skills in language, numeracy, creativity, and social development. At this level, assessments are designed to be child-friendly and activity-based, encouraging students to learn through observation, participation, and simple evaluations. Teachers assess students through: Class participation, Oral assessments, Activity-based learning, Worksheets and simple evaluation. The emphasis is on concept understanding and skill development rather than formal examinations.

Preparatory Stage (Grade III – V)

In the Preparatory Stage, students begin to develop stronger academic skills and are gradually introduced to a structured examination pattern.

Middle School (Grade VI – VIII)

At the Middle School level, the academic curriculum becomes more detailed and requires deeper understanding of subjects. Assessments are designed to evaluate both conceptual clarity and application skills.

Secondary Stage (Grade IX – XII)

At the Secondary and Senior Secondary level, the academic curriculum becomes more advanced and follows the guidelines of CISCE. Assessments are designed to evaluate conceptual understanding, application skills, and exam readiness. Continuous assessment along with Term Examinations helps students prepare for the Board Examinations.

Board Examination Results

Our consistent record of 100% pass results in ICSE and ISC examinations.
